topic: A person accidentally sends a text to the wrong recipient
raw_output:
[story 1]

My jaw tightened as I stared at the screen, a hot flush creeping up my neck. I had meant to send a scathing reply to Sarah about the disastrous project timeline, but my thumb had slipped, landing squarely on Mark’s contact. A beat of absolute silence followed, punctuated only by the frantic drumming of my own pulse. I quickly minimized the chat window, my hands trembling slightly as I tried to regain composure. I just needed to delete it before anyone saw the sheer, unvarnished exasperation I’d typed out. The silence in my small apartment suddenly felt deafening, amplifying the frantic churning in my stomach.

[story 2]

"Seriously?" I muttered under my breath, the sound sharp and brittle. I leaned back in my chair, rubbing the bridge of my nose with two fingers, my eyes narrowed to slits. The message was sent. A complete, unfiltered torrent of frustration about the endless administrative nonsense had just landed in the inbox of my boss’s new intern. A wave of heat washed over my face, making my skin prickle. I felt a sudden, desperate urge to slam my laptop shut, to physically shove the device away as if the very act of typing had somehow poisoned the air. I waited, frozen, watching the little notification bubble glow, the anticipation of inevitable fallout making my breath catch in my throat.

[story 3]

I slammed my hand lightly on the desk, the small impact echoing in the quiet office. The sheer absurdity of what I’d just typed—a perfectly calibrated expression of weary annoyance—made my eyes water. It was supposed to be a private venting session, a release valve for the mounting pressure, not an accidental public declaration of my displeasure to the entire marketing team. My shoulders slumped momentarily, a heavy, defeated weight settling on my back. I snatched my phone, my fingers fumbling over the keyboard, the movement jerky and uncontrolled. I needed to undo this catastrophe before I could talk myself out of deleting it entirely.
